title: MDCAT Biology: Cell Structure Preview
summary: A compact sample of how premium notes will explain concepts, exam traps and MCQ strategy.

previewSections.heading: Core Concept
previewSections.body: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. In entry tests, cell questions usually check organelle function, membrane structure, enzyme activity and comparison between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

previewSections.heading: Exam Trap
previewSections.body: Do not confuse mitochondria with ribosomes. Mitochondria relate to ATP production, while ribosomes relate to protein synthesis.

previewSections.heading: Sample MCQ
previewSections.body: Which organelle is directly linked with protein synthesis? Answer: Ribosome.

title: FAST Mathematics: Percentages Preview
summary: A short preview of formula use, exam shortcuts and common percentage mistakes.

previewSections.heading: Core Formula
previewSections.body: Percentage change = change divided by original value multiplied by 100. Always compare with the original value, not the final value.

previewSections.heading: Exam Shortcut
previewSections.body: For a 20% increase followed by a 20% decrease, the answer is not zero. Use 100 -> 120 -> 96, so the net result is a 4% decrease.

previewSections.heading: Sample MCQ
previewSections.body: A value rises from 80 to 100. Percentage increase = 20/80 x 100 = 25%.

title: NAT/GAT Analytical Reasoning Preview
summary: A sample note showing how analytical reasoning should be converted into symbols, tables and elimination.

previewSections.heading: Core Method
previewSections.body: Read the fixed conditions first, then create a clean table. Mark definite positions before testing flexible clues.

previewSections.heading: Exam Trap
previewSections.body: Students often solve from memory after one clue. In analytical reasoning, every new condition can change the arrangement.

previewSections.heading: Sample MCQ
previewSections.body: If A is before B and B is before C, then A must be before C. This is a definite inference.

title: GRE/SAT Vocabulary In Context Preview
summary: A sample vocabulary note built around context, contrast signals and exam-style sentence logic.

previewSections.heading: Core Method
previewSections.body: Do not memorise only the dictionary meaning. Look for contrast words, cause-effect signals and tone clues in the sentence.

previewSections.heading: Exam Trap
previewSections.body: A familiar word may be used in an unfamiliar context. The sentence meaning should control the answer.

previewSections.heading: Sample MCQ
previewSections.body: Although the task looked simple, it was actually arduous. Arduous means difficult and tiring.
